project_manifest.name: Universal Office
project_manifest.operational_status: Menaion corpus expansion active: November (30/30), December (31/31), January (31/31), February (28/28), March (31/31), April (30/30) imported; May–October remain. Horologion v2.1 engine + Menaion v1.5 corpus/resolver. Feast-rank override logic still deferred. Generic sidebar shell (#generic-settings) implemented as fourth sidebar identity.

open_bugs: 1. Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns. (LOW — see known_outstanding_issues.rite_placeholder_resolution)
architectural_debt: 1. Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js needs transition to a template system.

2. [CLOSED — v2.8.6] Hydration-race gating: renderOffice() is guarded by isHydrationComplete. selectMode() awaits hydration before setting that flag and calling requestRender(). requestRender()/flushRender() coalesce and serialise renders via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration. Residual: no automated regression test covers this gating path.
3. Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri/Kyrie) should be normalized into common.json.

4. Saints data model: identities.json + commemorations.json are the canonical source-of-truth. js/saints-resolver.js provides the shared resolver boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete (v2.8.8): 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 identities remain. COE audit and governance complete (v2.8.9). Remaining: normalized-source runtime adapter (SaintsResolver reads monthly cache files at runtime; a true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly).

5. [CLOSED — v2.8.9] COE saint/commemoration rendering: Three-layer rebuild complete. Layer 1 (season engine) was already operational. Layer 2 (fixed feasts: Sawma Rabba Friday martyrs, pre-Sawma Commemoration of the Faithful Departed) implemented in COE-IIA via getDayClass() in calendar-east-syriac.js. Layer 3 (sparse individual saints) source-data corrected via COE-IIB audit (133 IDs removed), eligibility gate implemented in js/coe-eligibility.js (CoeEligibility.filter()), and display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). saint-grid model eliminated. CoeEligibility.filter() is mandatory before any Layer 3 render. Layer 2 output from getDayClass() renders before Layer 3 lookup. No saint-grid fallback. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.

6. EO old/new calendar mode is partially implemented in admin-only tooling (CalendarEngine.getEOSeasonRanges() + admin/admin.html snapshots), but the main application still has no EO mode UI and does not load js/calendar-eastern-orthodox.js on the production path.
resolved_in_this_version: 1. COE saint section silenced: renderEastSyriac() saint display locked to empty pending architectural rebuild. Previous behaviour was historically inaccurate — string-matching tradition fields was surfacing wrong saints. Silence is liturgically correct for COE weekdays with no fixed saint.

resolved_in_this_version: 2. Saints resolver boundary (v2.8.5–2.8.6): js/saints-resolver.js introduced as shared module (SaintsResolver). office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through it. resolveCommemorations() is now a pure delegation wrapper. OOR inline fallback filters replaced with SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(). appData.saints eliminated. No direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js.

3. Hydration-race gating (v2.8.6 — doc closure): The scheduler/gating layer is implemented and verified. renderOffice() returns early unless isHydrationComplete is true. selectMode() awaits the correct hydration function before setting isHydrationComplete = true and calling requestRender(). requestRender()/flushRender() coalesce concurrent requests and serialise renders via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration. The original architectural debt note is closed as implemented.

No Byzantine logic lives in the UI layer.

js/calendar-engine.js.role: All date and liturgical calendar logic
js/calendar-engine.js.status: Current — v2.8.9 + 2026-03-06 admin EO diagnostics patch
js/calendar-engine.js.architecture_note: v2.8.1: Hardcoded SEASON_RANGES array (was valid through Dec 2030 only) replaced with algorithmic generator. getLiturgicalYear() is now fully algorithmic. Both are valid for any year indefinitely. Admin/devtool extension now adds EO snapshot helpers for old_calendar vs new_calendar diagnostics; this does not by itself expose EO mode in the main app.
public_methods: init() — loads bcp-propers.json; must be called before fetchLectionaryData()
getSeasonAndFile(date) — returns { season, file, liturgicalColor, litYear }
getLiturgicalYear(date) — returns 'year1' or 'year2'; algorithmic, anchored to Advent 2025 = Year 2
getCurrentProper(date) — returns Proper number 3–29
fetchLectionaryData(date) — returns daily entry object from seasonal JSON
getCurrentDate()
setCurrentDate(date)
changeDate(days)
resetDate()
formatDateForLookup(date) — returns 'February 17, 2026'
formatDateISO(date) — returns '2026-02-17'
getYearSnapshot(year, config) — admin/devtool snapshot adapter; routes EOR to EasternOrthodoxCalendar when available
getEOSeasonRanges(year, eoMode) — admin/devtool EO fixed-feast + season-range diagnostics for new_calendar vs old_calendar
private_methods: _getEaster(year) — Anonymous Gregorian algorithm; returns JS Date of Easter Sunday for any year
_getAdventSunday(year) — returns JS Date of Advent Sunday (Sunday on or after Nov 27) for any year
_generateSeasonRanges(year) — generates all season range objects for the liturgical year beginning in Nov/Dec of `year`
_getRangesForDate(date) — returns the correct generated range set for any date; checks prior and current calendar year
_julianToGregorianOffset(year) — century-based Julian→Gregorian offset helper for EO fixed-feast diagnostics
_isoFromDate(date) — local-time-safe YYYY-MM-DD formatter used by EO diagnostics

season_files.epiphany.json: Epiphany Jan 6 through day before Ash Wednesday
season_files.ordinary1.json: Day after Pentecost through Jul 31 (also: brief Ordinary Time between Epiphany and Ash Wednesday when applicable)
season_files.ordinary3.json: Oct 1 through day before Advent Sunday

js/calendar-engine.js.backward_compatibility: SEASON_RANGES static getter retained — returns algorithmically generated ranges for current and prior calendar year. Any external code referencing CalendarEngine.SEASON_RANGES continues to work.

js/calendar-ethiopian.js.role: Ethiopian (Ge'ez) calendar conversion and date resolution
js/calendar-ethiopian.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — Phase 7.4. Fully integrated.
public_methods: getEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns { day, month, monthIndex, year } in Amete Mihret era
getCopticDate(gregorianDate) — same algorithm, Coptic month names, EC-276 era
formatEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns display string e.g. '15 Yekatit 2018'
isEthiopianLeapYear(ethiopianYear) — boolean; leap when year % 4 === 3
MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Ge'ez month name strings
COPTIC_MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Coptic month name strings
implementation_notes: 1. Julian Day Number (JDN) method — valid across all historical and future dates
2. Epoch: 1 Meskerem 1 EC = JDN 1724221 = 29 August 8 AD (Julian)
3. Pagume: 5 days normally, 6 in leap years (year % 4 === 3)
4. Self-test suite on load — 9 verified reference points logged to console

js/horologion-engine.js.role: Byzantine Horologion office engine — skeleton loading, payload resolution, validation
public_methods: getOfficeSkeleton(officeKey, traditionVariant) — fetches and caches data/horologion/<officeKey>.json
resolveOffice(date, officeKey, context) — returns normalized payload; non-throwing; status:'error' on all failure paths

js/horologion-engine.js.architecture_note: Engine layer only — no rendering logic. Non-throwing contract: all failure modes return status:'error' payload. _formatLocalISODate() uses getFullYear/getMonth/getDate to avoid UTC date-shift. office-ui.js contains only a thin adapter (renderHorologionOffice, _renderHorologionItem) that walks the normalized payload — no Byzantine logic in the UI layer. v2.1: _resolveTroparionSlot() is now async and accepts dateObj as third parameter. Weekday (Mon–Fri) branch queries window.MenaionResolver.queryTroparion(mmdd) before falling through to the weekday-theme rubric. Bright Week, Saturday, and Sunday branches are unchanged. try/catch wraps the resolver call — non-throwing. MenaionResolver is a soft dependency: if the module is absent or the query fails, the weekday-theme fallback rubric renders as before (resolvedAs:'weekday-theme-rubric'). Call site in _resolveVespersSlots() awaits _resolveTroparionSlot() with dateObj.

js/menaion-resolver.js.role: Menaion pilot resolver — query boundary between the Horologion engine and Menaion date corpora
public_methods: queryTroparion(mmdd) — Promise<ResultObject>. Accepts 'MM-DD' string. Returns status + troparion text (if resolved) or fallback code. Non-throwing.
isMonthImported(monthNum) — boolean. Returns true only if the month's JSON has been registered in MONTH_FILES.
importedMonths() — returns array of imported month numbers.
result_status_codes: menaion-resolved — text found; full troparion text + name + tone + rank returned
menaion-text-unavailable — commemoration identified but troparion text not yet in corpus
menaion-not-imported — month has not been imported into the pilot corpus
menaion-no-ranked-commemoration — date imported but no commemoration meets minimum rank threshold
menaion-load-error — JSON fetch failed; engine falls through to theme rubric
js/menaion-resolver.js.architecture_note: IIFE pattern (same as HorologionEngine). Soft dependency: if this file fails to load or the query throws, horologion-engine.js degrades silently to the weekday-theme rubric. This module owns all Menaion data loading; the engine owns none. Rank selection rule: if multiple commemorations exist on a date, the highest-rank entry (lowest integer) is selected; ties resolved by first entry. CURRENT SCOPE: November (30/30), December (31/31), January (31/31), February (28/28), March (31/31), April (30/30) imported. All other months return 'menaion-not-imported'. No full annual Menaion coverage is claimed or implied.

schema.json.coverage: Documents the canonical shape for all future monthly Menaion files. Commemoration shape: id, name, type, rank, troparion_tone, troparion { tone, title, text }, troparion_status, tradition_tags, notes. Resolution contract codes documented.

hydration_race_condition.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.6 (doc closure). The hydration-race gating layer is implemented and verified in code. renderOffice() returns early unless isHydrationComplete is true. selectMode() awaits the correct hydration function before setting isHydrationComplete = true and calling requestRender(). requestRender() / flushRender() coalesce concurrent render requests and serialise execution via activeRender. init() is kernel-only and no longer silently triggers Daily Office hydration.
hydration_race_condition.residual_note: No automated regression test covers the isHydrationComplete gating path. A future test pass should add coverage to guard against silent regressions if the boot sequence is refactored.

coe_calendar_model.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.9. Three-layer COE calendar model implemented. Layer 1: season engine (calendar-east-syriac.js getSeason() / getDayClass()) operational. Layer 2: getDayClass() returns commemorations[] for Sawma Rabba Fridays and pre-Sawma Faithful Departed. Layer 3: CoeEligibility.filter() allowlist gate implemented; display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). COE-IIB audit complete: 133 identity IDs (136 rows) removed from commemorations.json. saint-grid model eliminated. No saint-grid fallback. Layer 2 renders before Layer 3 lookup. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.
coe_calendar_model.architectural_principle: Latin logic: Calendar = Saints framework with seasonal overlay. East Syriac logic: Calendar = Salvation-historical seasons with occasional saints.

layer_2_fixed_feasts.description: Major fixed observances calculated seasonally, not stored as date-keyed saints.

Saints included only because they are 'early enough'
Gregory the Great — disputed, currently tagged COE, needs review
Any saint tagged COE solely by proximity to EOR
layer_3_individual_saints.action: No further internal action required. One external-research holdout remains: mar-augustine / mar-augustine-commemoration — excluded from Layer 3 by the eligibility gate. Do not alter on internal evidence alone. Resolution requires external Syriac manuscript evidence (Budge Book of the Governors, Wright Catalogue, Encyclopaedia Iranica COE calendar). If a matching Hudra figure is confirmed: correct feast dates, merge identity records, add to allowlist. If no figure is found: REMOVE_COE_TAG from both records.

season_weekly_logic.subara: Text-heavy, saint-light. Marian material doxological. No Immaculate Conception construct.

season_weekly_logic.sawma_rabba: Friday = Commemoration of the Martyrs (corporate). No rotating individual saint list. Inject dynamically.
season_weekly_logic.qyamta: Apostolic figures more visible. Thomas receives liturgical prominence. Paschal weeks suppress martyr themes.
season_weekly_logic.shlihe: Apostles commemorated strongly. Missionary saints possible (pre-schism). Individual apostle entries appropriate.
season_weekly_logic.eliya_sliwa: Prophetic focus. Prophets and OT figures appropriate.

horologion_variable_engine.description: Horologion Vespers variable slots — status after Menaion v1 pilot (engine v2.1). Every original skeleton slot now yields full text, a tone-identified rubric stub, or an explicit omission rubric across all day-of-week paths. Saturday Great Vespers is substantively complete for ordinary weeks. Sunday Small Vespers baseline is implemented. Weekday dismissal Theotokion (Mon–Fri) is materially resolved from the Octoechos weekday corpus (v1.9). Weekday troparion-or-apolytikion (Mon–Fri) has a live Menaion-backed path (Menaion v1.2 / engine v2.1): on imported dates (November 30/30, December 31/31, January 31/31, February 28/28, March 31/31, April 30/30) the slot resolves to full text (type:'text', resolvedAs:'menaion-resolved') or a named rubric (resolvedAs:'menaion-text-unavailable') where text is unconfirmed; on all other dates it falls back to the weekday-theme rubric (resolvedAs:'weekday-theme-rubric'). No full annual Menaion coverage is claimed. The slot is not 'unresolved' — the remaining work is corpus expansion (May–October). Slot summary: stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried: all paths resolved (v1.3–v1.8). aposticha: all paths resolved. kathisma-reading: Mon–Sat appointment rubric (v1.5); Sunday omission rubric (v1.8); Bright Week omitted. daily-prokeimenon: all days resolved (v1.2). vesperal-reading: omitted all ordinary days (v1.2). troparion-or-apolytikion: Saturday + Bright Week + Sunday full text (v1.6–v1.8); Mon–Fri pilot-path-active (Menaion v1). theotokion-dismissal: all paths resolved (v1.7–v1.9). Remaining refinement: Menaion corpus expansion (May–October unimported; November/December/January/February/March/April complete). Feast-rank override system. Great Lent variable overrides. Kathisma full psalm text.
horologion_variable_engine.action: Next: Menaion corpus expansion — continue monthly corpus files (May is next). After corpus: feast-rank override engine. Weekday Theotokion is resolved (v1.9); weekday troparion pilot path is live (Menaion v1) — do not describe either as 'stub-only' or 'unresolved'.

recent_logic_changes.change: Menaion v1 pilot wired into live weekday Vespers troparion resolution. Engine version v2.1.
recent_logic_changes.detail: NEW FILES: js/menaion-resolver.js — IIFE module (window.MenaionResolver). Soft dependency on horologion-engine.js. Exposes queryTroparion(mmdd) → Promise<ResultObject>. MONTH_FILES map: only month 11 (november) active. _loadMonth(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, result cached. _selectBestCommemoration(): highest rank (lowest integer); ties go to first entry. Result status codes: menaion-resolved, menaion-text-unavailable, menaion-not-imported, menaion-no-ranked-commemoration, menaion-load-error. data/menaion/schema.json: pilot schema contract for all future monthly Menaion files. data/menaion/november.json: 12 of 30 November dates imported (11-01, 11-08, 11-09, 11-13, 11-14, 11-16, 11-17, 11-20, 11-21, 11-22, 11-25, 11-30). Source: OCA/Antiochian Hapgood-tradition English; Jordanville Horologion. CHANGED FILES: index.html — js/menaion-resolver.js added before js/horologion-engine.js. js/horologion-engine.js — version bumped v2.0→v2.1. _resolveTroparionSlot(): made async; third parameter dateObj added. Bright Week, Saturday, Sunday branches: UNCHANGED. Weekday (Mon–Fri) branch: builds MM-DD string from dateObj; calls window.MenaionResolver.queryTroparion(mmdd); routes on status: menaion-resolved → type:'text' with full troparion text + saint name + tone + rank annotation (resolvedAs:'menaion-resolved'); menaion-text-unavailable → type:'rubric' with commemoration name (resolvedAs:'menaion-text-unavailable'); all other statuses or resolver unavailable → falls through to weekday-theme rubric (resolvedAs:'weekday-theme-rubric', identical to v2.0 fallback). try/catch wraps entire resolver call — non-throwing. typeof window.MenaionResolver guard prevents crash if module not loaded. Call site in _resolveVespersSlots(): _resolveTroparionSlot() awaited with dateObj. _resolveVespersSlots() was already async — no signature change. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES. PILOT SCOPE: November only (12 of 30 dates). All other months return 'menaion-not-imported'. GOVERNANCE CONSEQUENCE: The framing 'weekday troparion is simply unresolved' is retired. After Menaion v1, weekday troparion has a live pilot path. Imported November dates resolve to full text. Non-imported dates fall back honestly to the theme rubric. Remaining work is corpus expansion + feast-rank engine. 'Menaion required' now refers to corpus expansion, not a missing architecture.

Each entry: tone, weekday, title, full liturgical text. Monday/Tuesday/Thursday: standard Octoechos weekday Theotokion for that tone + day. Wednesday/Friday: Stavrotheotokion (Cross Theotokion) — standard Byzantine fasting-day practice. js/horologion-engine.js: version bumped v1.8→v1.9. WEEKDAY_THEOTOKION_URL constant added. _weekdayTheotokionData cache variable added. _loadWeekdayTheotokionData(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, graceful degradation — if data file fails, falls back to v1.7-style tone-identified rubric stub (not a crash). _resolveTheotokionSlot() weekday branch (Mon–Fri): upgraded from tone-identified rubric stub (resolvedAs:'weekday-menaion-required') to type:'text' with full Octoechos weekday Theotokion text (resolvedAs:'weekday-octoechos-theotokion'). Limitation recorded in each output: baseline uses current Octoechos weekly tone; if Menaion troparion is of a different tone the Theotokion should follow that tone — not yet implemented; stated openly. _capitalise() internal helper added for weekday name formatting in rubric text. Promise.all in _resolveVespersSlots: five-loader → six-loader (adds _loadWeekdayTheotokionData()). Saturday, Sunday, and Bright Week theotokion paths: UNCHANGED. troparion-or-apolytikion (Mon–Fri): UNCHANGED — remains tone-identified rubric stub; Menaion data is genuinely required; no fabrication. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES — type:'text' already handled. GOVERNANCE CONSEQUENCE: The framing 'weekday Menaion refinement for troparion + theotokion' is now incorrect. After v1.9, the only remaining open weekday slot is troparion-or-apolytikion (Mon–Fri); theotokion-dismissal is baseline-resolved. saints_model.status_note: All 12 monthly cache files are present as of v2.8.2 and last regenerated in v2.8.8 (Phase 4 duplicate consolidation). js/saints-resolver.js (SaintsResolver) is the sole monthly-cache owner and the canonical saints boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. No appData.saints usage or direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js. Phase 4 duplicate identity consolidation complete: 46 pairs resolved, 1,228 unique identities remain.
saints_model.workflow: Edit identities.json or commemorations.json → run `npm run saints:regen` (validates then regenerates all months) → commit modified source files and all regenerated monthly cache files together. To regenerate a single month: `node tools/build_saints_cache.js <month>`.
rules: Do not edit saints-<month>.json files manually.
All dates in source-of-truth files must use numeric { month, day }.
Tradition is defined only in commemorations.json.
ECU is derived by generator when identity has commemorations in all five traditions on the same date.
Generated cache records carry a `type` field derived from identity.type. Do not flatten type distinctions (saint / feast / commemoration / apostle / prophet / marian_feast) at the UI layer.
unfinished_work: Normalized-source runtime adapter: SaintsResolver reads generated cache files at runtime. A true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly.

todos.title: Horologion Vespers: Menaion corpus expansion and feast-rank engine
todos.description: IN PROGRESS — Menaion v1.2 active (2026-03-07). Live Menaion path active for Mon–Fri weekday troparion. November (30/30), December (31/31), January (31/31), February (28/28), March (31/31), and April (30/30) imported and resolving. Non-imported months fall back to weekday-theme rubric. No full annual Menaion coverage is claimed or present. Remaining work: (a) Menaion corpus expansion — continue monthly corpus files (May next, then June–October). The architecture (MenaionResolver + monthly JSON schema) is proven; expansion is a data-import pass. (b) Feast-rank override system — proper feast apolytikia and Theotokia require a feast-rank engine that compares Menaion rank to Octoechos defaults; deferred beyond corpus. (c) Menaion-tone Theotokion conflict — when Menaion troparion tone differs from Octoechos weekly tone, dismissal Theotokion should follow Menaion tone; not yet implemented. (d) Great Lent overrides and kathisma full psalm text — lowest priority. All other Vespers variable slots are resolved: Saturday, Sunday, Bright Week, weekday stichera/aposticha/kathisma/prokeimenon/theotokion all have full text or explicit rubric/omission.

todos.title: Horologion: Compline (Small Apodeipnon) v1
todos.description: Add data/horologion/compline.json skeleton. Small Apodeipnon structure: Usual Beginning, fixed Psalms (50, 69, 142, Doxology), Creed, Trisagion, Our Father, troparia, Kontakion of the day (variable — placeholder), Prayer of St. Basil, dismissal. Most content is fixed; only Kontakion slot is variable.

todos.title: Horologion: Little Hours v1 (First, Third, Sixth, Ninth)
todos.description: Add skeleton files for the four Little Hours. Fixed structure per hour: Usual Beginning, fixed Psalms, Theotokion (variable — placeholder), Kontakion (variable — placeholder), Prayer, Dismissal. Fixed Psalm assignments: First Hour (5, 89, 100), Third Hour (16, 24, 50), Sixth Hour (53, 54, 90), Ninth Hour (83, 84, 85). Implement as four separate skeleton JSON files.

todos.title: Horologion: Orthros (Matins) v1 — DEFERRED, high complexity
todos.description: DEFERRED — do not begin until Octoechos tone engine is stable from Vespers work. Orthros is the most structurally complex Byzantine office: two variable kathisma readings per day (Psalter weekly cycle), Canon of 9 odes with variable troparia per tone/week/feast, Lauds Psalms (148–150), variable Exapostilarion, variable Ainoi stichera. Blocked on: horologion-vespers-variable-slots completion.
